mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced present value of future cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A month-over-month increase in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% in June 2008, near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market stress
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, this signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427k units in May 2008, declining from prior peaks
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) relative to individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. Declining INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and equity valuations. A 1% month-over-month decline in INDPRO predicts negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) is a high-frequency labor market indicator. Sustained month-over-month declines in PAYEMS signal labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative equity returns. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in more than 55% of months with declining PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.0% YoY signals monetary pressure that constrains central bank easing capacity. When inflation is elevated, equity investors expect sustained high discount rates, producing negative 60-day forward returns even if nominal rates decline.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.11 in June 2008, elevated inflation environment
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 52% of instances when inflation exceeds 3.0% YoY, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture aggregate demand momentum. When GDPC1 growth slows below 2% annualized AND RSAFS declines, this signals demand-side recession risk that produces negative 60-day equity returns through earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS declines MoM
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20054.66B in Q1 2008, growth decelerating
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of jointly triggered instances, or interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead broader economic activity by 3-6 months. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1400k units signals housing market collapse that predicts negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprice recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427k in May 2008, approaching critical threshold
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 52% of instances when HOUST < 1400k, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.
